title: Kissai

The '''Kissai''' were a subspecies of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]] who were enslaved by [[Exiles|exiled]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. The Kissai were the priest class in the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] caste system, where the Kissai would study the nature of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and practice ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]] and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]].

==Biology and appearance==
Like most members of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]], the Kissai were [[red]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with distinctly sharp, predatory features<ref name="KOTORCG" /> and tendrils on both sides of their chins.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref>

==History==
As a subspecies of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] species, the ancient Kissai presumably originated on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Since Sith society lasted as such for hundreds of centuries—from roughly [[100,000 BBY/Legends|100,000 BBY]], it is presumed that the Kissai also had an ancient history. Amongst the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith]] the Kissai became the priests and spiritual leaders who would indoctrinate others in the teachings of the Sith.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

Except for the brief unification of their species under [[King/Legends|King]] [[Adas]] in his [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|empire]], Sith society was fragmented, with many individual Sith Lords ruling independent fiefdoms, most of them attempting to claim Adas's title of [[Sith'ari/Legends|Sith'ari]] and unite their kind. When the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] and Kissai fled Korriban to resettle on [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] after Adas' [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] [[Invasion of Korriban]],<ref name="tsd">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> the Kissai oversaw the rebuilding efforts.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

[[Kissai heretics|Some Kissai priests]] interpreted the death of their Sith'ari during the Invasion of Korriban as a sign to reject the Sith point of view of the dark side of the Force. These heretics were banished from the Sith homeworld, following their instincts to the Force-soaked world [[Tund/Legends|Tund]].<ref name="KOTORCGWE5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/KOTORweben5|text=KOTOR Campaign Guide Web Enhancement 5: Karnak Tetsu, Sorcerer of Tund}}</ref>
[[File:Kissai_1.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A Kissai watches Ludo Kressh and Naga Sadow fight during the Funeral of Marka Ragnos.]]

Following the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> this pure-blooded Sith society became the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]], combining science, ontology, and sorcery. Convinced by their Rakatan interactions that all [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] are [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], and discarding the Siths' view of the Force, the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]] proclaimed that the omnipresence of the Force or "the Unity" illuminates the deception of dualities and multiplicity. Their cosmology viewed life as perfectly harmonious and the existence of true opposites as an illusion.<ref name="KOTORCGWE5" />

The sorcerers had interactions with early [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], who viewed them as merely another Force sect, like the [[Jal Shey]] or [[Zeison Sha]]. However, following the Restoration—and [[Karnak Tetsu]]'s reign of terror after the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]—the Jedi labeled the sorcerers' beliefs as unqualified heresy. The sorcerers were reticent in accepting outlanders, believing that only true Sith can grasp their truth. On one occasion, however, they welcomed the few remaining [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] and Kissai, descended from outcasts much like themselves.<ref name="KOTORCGWE5" />

These descendants of Kissai who fled to Tund continued to live on through the many centuries until [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]; when the [[Croke]] [[Rokur Gepta]] learned all he could from the Sorcerers and then he slew them all using the [[electromagnetic torpedo]]. The green energies contained in the torpedo [[Destruction of Tund|killed every life form on the planet]].<ref name="TEA" />
[[File:Sorcerer_of_Tund_EGF.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The Sorcerers of Tund were descendants of exiled Kissai.]]

However, the descendants of those Kissai who did not get exiled and instead fled to Ziost after the death of Adas were eventually [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] and absorbed into the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] after the [[Discovery of Korriban|arrival]] of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]] on Korriban in [[6900 BBY]]. They believed that [[Ajunta Pall]] was a manifestation of [[Typhojem/Legends|Typhojem]].<ref name="BOS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref> Unlike Massassi, who were transformed into soldiers for various Sith Lords, Kissai in the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] were largely left on Ziost as the servants of other [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] and several, such as [[Marka Ragnos]] and [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], reached very high positions in the Empire due to their priest status.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

After the end of the Great Hyperspace war and the fall of the Sith Empire, Kissai as well as Massassai were not often found in Republic space. If any adventurers of these subspecies did happen to travel within Republic space, most beings in the galaxy would not recognize a Kissai or Massassi at first glance, even though members of these subspecies generally draw attention to themselves through their appearance.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

The Kissai have been largely unheard-of since the acceptance of the Kissai and Massassi into the Sorcerers of Tund after Great Sith War and reports of isolated villages of both Kissai and Massassi on Korriban and Tund, although there is no record of them and the rest of the Sith species ever becoming extinct.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==Society and culture==
Kissai were loyal and capable allies who preferred to work with others, making them excellent aides and assistants. They responded well to instruction and were adaptable despite great adversity. The Kissai were just as ruthless as the Massassi, but they were more intelligent and observant. Their strict cultural standards demanded that they serve their masters well, a trait instilled in them by the Dark Jedi who first enslaved their species.<ref name="KOTORCG" />
